description: A dish of finely chopped or minced raw beef seasoned with ingredients such as shallots, capers, mustard, pepper, and Worcestershire sauce. It is often served with or topped by a raw egg yolk.
preparation_steps: Select very fresh beef suitable for raw preparation and keep it cold.
Trim and hand-chop or finely mince the beef.
Mix or serve with finely chopped seasonings such as shallot, capers, and condiments.
Shape the tartare neatly and add egg yolk if using.

health_advice.benefits: High in protein and often low in carbohydrates.
health_advice.risks: The major concern is foodborne illness from raw beef and raw egg if used.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Only prepare it with trusted ingredients under strict hygiene and cold-chain control; many people should avoid raw-meat dishes altogether.

dietary_notes.note: Naturally meat-based. It can be gluten-free if sauces used are gluten-free.

allergens: Egg if egg yolk is used
Possible gluten in Worcestershire or other sauces

serving_info.typical_serving: A small molded portion as an appetizer or a larger portion as a light main
